heroHeadline: Oil Crashes 10% on Hormuz Opening; S&P 500 Hits Record 7,000; FOMC Apr 29 Looms
heroSubtitle: Apr 17: Iran FM Araghchi declared the Strait of Hormuz 'fully open' to commercial traffic, crashing WTI crude 9.6% to ~$86 — the largest single-day drop since the Apr 7 ceasefire. Apr 15: S&P 500 broke 7,000 (record 7,022.95, first-ever close) and Nasdaq posted its 12th consecutive winning session. Apr 16: China Q1 2026 GDP beat at 5.0% (vs 4.8% forecast), but March exports slowed sharply to +2.5%. IMF WEO Apr 14 confirmed global growth at 3.1% (adverse scenario: 2.5%). ECB markets now price a rate hike to 2.5% by year-end — a dramatic reversal. Powell's Fed Chair term expires May 15 with no successor named. FOMC April 29-30 stagflation decision: core PCE 3.06%, Michigan sentiment at all-time low 47.6, but oil crash may reduce near-term CPI pressure.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO Apr 2026, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Conference Board, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, IEA, EIA, China NBS, Atlantic Council
